reasoning: Tajima Beef (GI #2) and Kobe Beef (GI #3) appear first with official MAFF descriptions explaining the breeding relationship. All facts match official GI registrations. However the 195-item unfiltered list buries signal under unrelated Hyogo foods.
missing_data: Specific restaurants or farms in Tajima region where visitors can eat/see Tajima beef. Context on when Tajima beef became GI registered vs Kobe beef.
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level filtering or a keyword parameter so 'Tajima beef' queries can surface GI #2 and #3 without 193 unrelated items. Alternatively rank by relevance to the query term.

reasoning: Arabic translations of Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef appear first with accurate official descriptions. Translation quality is high (confidence=high). Same structural issue as L1-01: 195 items returned with no ranking toward the specific query.
missing_data: Same as English version: no visitor access info (farms, restaurants). Arabic transliteration of Awajishima torafugu name ('أواجيشيما') is phonetic but acceptable.
improvement_hint: Implement keyword-based relevance scoring so the first items returned best match the query string, reducing cognitive load for agent and end user.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, not Kyoto city monuments. get_japan_heritage returns Nijo Castle, Toji, Nishi Honganji—all Kyoto city landmarks with no relation to Minami-Yamashiro. The scope_note even warns Kumano Kodo etc. should use search_area instead.
missing_data: Any data about Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村), its tea fields (茶畑), or Yamashiro tea GI designation. Should have used search_area({'q':'南山城 茶畑'}) or get_local_food with keyword.
improvement_hint: Route sub-prefectural place + product queries through search_area or get_local_food with keyword filter rather than prefecture-level get_japan_heritage.

reasoning: Yoshida Fire Festival appears first with correct Spanish name and Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property designation. However description_en=null, date=null, and the second item (神明の花火大会) is an unrelated fireworks festival. National heritage shows non-Yamanashi items. Enough to serve the intent but data quality is thin.
missing_data: Festival date (late August 26-27), description of the torch-lighting ceremony on Mt Fuji's north face, connection to Sengen Shrine. The bunca_id is null—Bunka-cho link would improve traceability.
improvement_hint: Enrich bunka_intangible records with date, detailed description, and the official Bunka-cho URL so festivals return actionable planning info.

reasoning: Query asks about Chuugi-region yuzu. 物部ゆず (GI #96) appears first—a different Kochi yuzu GI, not 中芸ゆず (GI #183). The second item is scraped nav-chrome (観光スポット検索) with no food content. Response truncated at 500/685; the target GI likely buried. Signal exists but ranking fails.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず (GI #183) should rank first. Production area: Kochi's Nahari, Yasuda, Kitagawa, Umaji, Geisei municipalities. Yuzu processing products and visitor info.
improvement_hint: Apply keyword/entity matching before ranking so 中芸ゆず rises to top when the query contains '中芸'. Filter scraped nav-chrome items (empty description_ja) from food listings.

reasoning: Chinese query uses get_spots without lang=zh, so names appear in Japanese. Hirosaki Castle and Hirosaki Park (sakura_meisho_100, must_see) return first with correct coordinates, website, phone. Hirosaki Park description confirms sakura relevance. Good factual coverage for the query.
missing_data: Bloom timing (typically late April–early May), cherry tree count (~2,600), moat boat rentals. Chinese-language descriptions absent since lang not set to zh.
improvement_hint: Ensure lang parameter is passed correctly from the query language so Chinese users receive Chinese descriptions rather than Japanese. Add seasonal metadata (sakura bloom window) to sakura_meisho spots.

reasoning: Korean query with lang=ko returns Izumo Taisha as first result with National Treasure designation, coordinates, opening hours, website, fee=no. Honden also included. Korean name fields appear to be absent (name_ko not shown in truncated response) but structurally well formed. 86 results total including the station.
missing_data: Korean-language descriptions for the shrine. Context on the October 'Kamiari-zuki' gathering of gods, which is central to Izumo Taisha's significance.
wrong_data: Station (Izumo Taisha-mae Station) tagged as shinto_shrine in kinds—minor data error.
improvement_hint: Fix kinds for Izumo Taisha-mae Station (remove shinto_shrine, add railway_station). Add Korean-language descriptions for top attractions.

reasoning: French query but tool passes lang=en. Itsukushima island and shrine return first with UNESCO WHS designation, National Treasure, correct coordinates. Otorii gate and west corridor also included. Factually excellent. French traveler gets English content only.
missing_data: Ferry timing from Hiroshima/Miyajima ferry access, tide schedule (for walking to torii at low tide), French-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: Pass the query's source language to the tool call. Add practical transport info (ferry access from Hiroshima port) to Miyajima/Itsukushima records.

reasoning: German query, tool scope_note warns Kumano Kodo should use search_area, yet get_japan_heritage returns Kumano Kodo (Q1148078) and Kumano Sanzan (Q1156100) in top_canonical_heritage with UNESCO + Historic Site designations and detailed descriptions. Good recovery. Lang=en not German.
missing_data: Trail route details (Nakahechi, Kohechi, Ohechi), trailhead access from Kii-Tanabe or Shingu, German-language content. Kongōbu-ji (Koyasan) appears first but isn't Kumano Kodo.
improvement_hint: Prioritise Kumano Kodo entry when query explicitly mentions 熊野古道 or Kumano Kodo, even in get_japan_heritage. Respect source language in lang parameter.

reasoning: Russian query, tool uses lang=en. Himeji Castle returns first with prominence_score=1, UNESCO + National Treasure + Special Historic Site designations, opening hours by season, phone, website, fee, wheelchair info. Sub-components (Ni/Ha/I/Ro Watariyagura) also present. Structurally excellent data.
missing_data: Russian-language descriptions. Admission price (currently just fee=yes, price_band=low). Cherry blossom season context (castle is sakura_meisho_100).
improvement_hint: Respect query language in lang parameter so Russian users receive Russian content. Surface admission price range in price_band more granularly.

reasoning: Thai query, Bunraku appears first with Thai name 'บุนราคุ' and UNESCO ICH (2003). Subsequent items (Kabuki, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku) are unrelated national items. No Osaka-specific venue info returned. The tool is national-scope; agent must supplement with venue lookup.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theatre (国立文楽劇場) address in Osaka, performance schedule/ticketing, Osaka context. Query specifically asked about Osaka.
improvement_hint: Link cultural heritage items to specific venues where the art is performed. For Bunraku, attach National Bunraku Theatre (Osaka) as a see_also venue.

reasoning: Vietnamese query asks about Yumihama Kasuri, a METI textile craft (弓浜絣). get_local_specialty returns 7 Tottori items but the visible items are all MAFF food GI (rakkyo, broccoli, kaki, watermelon). Yumihama Kasuri (METI craft) is likely in the 7 items but not visible in the 4000-char truncation. Ranking failure—food items surface before the targeted textile.
missing_data: Yumihama Kasuri description, production area (Sakaiminato/Yonago), weaving technique, where to see it. Should appear before food GI items when query is about a specific craft.
improvement_hint: When get_local_specialty is called without category filter, rank METI craft items above food GI if a craft name is in the query. Or the agent should pass category='craft'.

reasoning: Returns Benesse Art Site Naoshima (with website), Naoshima New Museum of Art with coordinates, municipality match, and scraped tourism association spots. Benesse missing coordinates but has website. Mixed-language scraped content (Traditional Chinese) appears. Good functional coverage for planning.
missing_data: Chichu Art Museum, Lee Ufan Museum, Art House Project (Honmura). Ferry access from Uno/Takamatsu. Individual museum entry fees and reservation requirements.
improvement_hint: Add Chichu Art Museum and Lee Ufan Museum as top wikidata entities for Naoshima. Coordinates for Benesse Art Site should be filled.

reasoning: Kakunodate samurai district (Q11428562) returns first with excellent description covering preservation district status, weeping cherry blossoms, coordinates, price_band, typical_visit_minutes=120, suitable_for including family/couple. Additional entities (Kakunodate town, weeping cherry trees, Hirafuku Museum) round out coverage.
missing_data: Individual samurai residence names (Aoyagi-ke, Ishiguro-ke) open to visitors and admission fees. Spring cherry blossom festival dates.
improvement_hint: Enrich Kakunodate buke_yashiki record with names of publicly accessible residences as sub-items or see_also links for more actionable planning.

reasoning: Returns Shimanami Kaido (cycling route), city museum, Onomichi city entity with Japan Heritage designation. Missing key Onomichi attractions: Senkoji Hill temple walk, Jodo-ji, the 'Cats Alley' slope path, and the town's film/literary associations. Coverage is sparse for the query.
missing_data: Senkoji Temple and ropeway, temple walk (25 temples), Onomichi old town slopes, cat alley, literary/cinema heritage. These are the main reasons visitors come to Onomichi.
improvement_hint: Enrich Onomichi municipal scrape with the temple walk route and Senkoji. The wikidata entities for Senkoji (Q11494060) and Jodo-ji should be included in the prefecture file.

reasoning: Nachi Falls returns first with correct coordinates (33.672, 135.891), Wakayama, Place of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monument designations. Related entities (Nachi Primeval Forest, 48 Waterfalls, Mount Nachi, Nachi Kogen Park) also returned. Comprehensive coverage for the query. Lang=en despite Spanish query.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha shrine context (adjacent to falls), Seiganto-ji pagoda (iconic photo with falls), Spanish-language descriptions. Access from JR Kii-Katsuura station.
wrong_data: 'Nachi Waterfall' (Q107045004) entry coordinates (35.66, 139.71) are in Tokyo—this is likely a painting, not the falls.
improvement_hint: Separate the Nachi Waterfall painting record from the actual waterfall to avoid confusion. Add Nachi Taisha and Seiganto-ji as see_also for this complex.

reasoning: Hindi query, returns Shiretoko (Q17208861), Shiretoko Peninsula (Q629238) with UNESCO WHS, Shiretoko National Park, Mount Io, World Heritage Center in sequence. Comprehensive coverage of the peninsula. 90 results total. Response uses lang=en rather than Hindi.
missing_data: Shiretoko Five Lakes access info, seasonal restrictions (wildlife/bear season Apr-Jul), cruise options from Utoro. Hindi-language descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal access restrictions to outdoor nature spots (e.g. Shiretoko Five Lakes bear season). Pass query language to the lang parameter.

reasoning: Dewa Sanzan (Q1155726) returns first with correct prefecture (Yamagata), Japan Heritage designation, sacred mountain kinds, typical_visit_minutes=240. Dewa Sanzan Shrine (Q120682042) also present. However second result (Tebajima island, Tokushima) is unrelated noise from the broad '出羽' search term.
missing_data: Individual mountains (Haguro-san, Gas-san, Yudono-san) and their access routes. Yamabushi ascetic practice context. Mountain hut/pilgrim lodging info.
improvement_hint: Disambiguate '出羽' search to prioritise Yamagata matches when context implies Dewa Sanzan. Add the three individual peaks as child entities linked to the Dewa Sanzan parent entity.

reasoning: Portuguese query. Yakushima (Q242176) returns first with UNESCO WHS, Wilderness Area, Ramsar, Protected Forest designations. Yakushima National Park, World Heritage Center, History and Folk Museum, Yakushima-taisha all follow. 94 results. Well-structured coverage.
missing_data: Jomon Sugi access route and trekking time (10h round trip), ferry schedule from Kagoshima, accommodation options in Anbo/Miyanoura. Portuguese-language descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add trekking route data (distance, difficulty, access season) to major hiking destinations like Yakushima. Respect query language in lang parameter.

reasoning: Korean query about Sado Island uses get_japan_heritage(Niigata). Returns Sado Gold Mine (UNESCO WHS 2024) prominently. Japan Heritage stories cover fire-clay culture, not Sado-specific content. The island's other features—Toki (crested ibis) conservation, Noh theater, Kodo taiko drummers—are entirely absent.
missing_data: Sado's Noh theatre tradition (world's densest concentration), Kodo Taiko drumming, toki/ibis reintroduction, Sado Kinzan mine visitor access. Tool returns mostly mainland Niigata items.
improvement_hint: For island queries, add search_area({'q':'佐渡島'}) as a complementary call to capture the full island profile beyond just the gold mine heritage designation.

reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku region onsen ryokan (6 prefectures). Tool only fetches Akita. Response returns valid 日本秘湯を守る会 onsen ryokan with official designation, but most lack coordinates, English names, and website. Coverage is Akita-only, missing Iwate, Aomori, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan from Iwate (Nyuto Onsen), Yamagata (Ginzan Onsen), Aomori, Miyagi, Fukushima. Coordinates and website for listed Akita ryokan. English names.
improvement_hint: For regional queries spanning multiple prefectures, call get_hotels for all relevant prefectures or implement a region parameter that expands to constituent prefectures automatically.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks for shukubo (temple lodgings) on the Shikoku pilgrimage. get_hotels(Tokushima) returns general OSM hotels and ryokan—none are identified as shukubo/temple accommodations. The pilgrimage-specific lodging type is entirely absent from the response. Complete intent mismatch.
missing_data: Temple lodgings (宿坊) at specific Shikoku 88 temples (e.g. Ryozen-ji #1, Gokurakuji #2 in Tokushima). Shukubo category data not yet in the corpus.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type category sourced from the Shikoku Henro 88 temple association official list. Until then, the tool should note it does not have shukubo data.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokkaido farm experience (農業体験). Tool returns get_spots (tourist attractions: volcano, clock tower, castle, mountains). No agritourism or farm-stay data present. Completely wrong tool and data type for this query. The corpus appears to lack agricultural experience data.
missing_data: Farm experience programs in Furano (lavender/melon), Biei, Tokachi (dairy). Agritourism operators, booking info. This data type is absent from the corpus.
improvement_hint: Add agritourism as a category to the scraper pipeline, targeting Hokkaido prefectural tourism sites and JA (agricultural cooperative) tourism pages. Alternatively, surface DMO-linked farm programs via get_dmo.

reasoning: Returns 3 Saga traditional craft items: Hakata Ori (textile), Imari-Arita Ware, Karatsu Ware. Both pottery entries directly answer 'traditional pottery in Kyushu.' Official METI designations, production areas, descriptions all accurate. see_also_wikidata_venues includes Takeo City Library/Museum. Query about pottery kilns—kiln visit info absent but core data strong.
missing_data: Kiln visit or pottery experience venues in Arita/Karatsu. Kyushu-wide context: Satsuma ware (Kagoshima), Hasami ware (Nagasaki) also notable. Hakata Ori is weaving, not pottery—it answers a slightly different question.
improvement_hint: Add see_also kiln/workshop venue links to METI craft records so travelers can find where to actually see pottery being made, not just read the official description.

reasoning: Korean query asks for Tohoku traditional festivals. Tool fetches only Aomori festivals (2 items: Hachinohe Sansha Taisai, Aomori Nebuta). national_heritage section includes non-Tohoku items (Dainichido Bugaku from Akita is Tohoku, but Kawagoe Hikawa and Uozu Tatemon are not). Missing Tanabata (Miyagi), Kanto (Akita), Sansa Odori (Iwate).
missing_data: Sendai Tanabata (Miyagi), Akita Kanto festival, Morioka Sansa Odori (Iwate), Yamagata Hanagasa festival. Need multi-prefecture calls for full Tohoku coverage.
improvement_hint: Implement a region parameter in get_festivals that expands to all prefectures in a given region (Tohoku=Aomori+Iwate+Miyagi+Akita+Yamagata+Fukushima) with merged and deduplicated results.
